title: Kolkata Kathi Roll

subs.name: store-bought parathas or chapatis save time; cook the egg on top of the ready bread
firm tofu pressed dry and seasoned the same way works well for a dairy-free filling
sriracha or any chilli hot sauce blended with a teaspoon of vinegar gives a similar kick

steps.text: Make the dough: mix wheat flour, a pinch of salt, and 1 tablespoon of oil in a bowl. Add water gradually, kneading to form a soft, smooth dough — slightly softer than chapati dough. Cover and rest for 15 minutes. Divide into 4 equal balls and roll each ball on a floured surface into a thin round about 22 cm in diameter.

Cook the paneer: heat a tablespoon of oil in a tawa or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add ginger-garlic paste and stir for 30 seconds. Add the paneer strips with red chilli powder, garam masala, half the chaat masala, and salt. Toss and cook for 3–4 minutes until the paneer is lightly browned on the edges. Add sliced capsicum and half the sliced onion and stir-fry for another 2 minutes so the vegetables are cooked but retain a little crunch. Remove from heat and squeeze half the lemon over the filling.

Cook the egg-coated paratha: heat a tawa over medium-high flame and drizzle with a little oil. Place one rolled paratha on the hot tawa and cook for 90 seconds until small bubbles appear and the underside is partially cooked. Flip the paratha. Beat one egg with a pinch of salt, pour it around the edges of the paratha on the tawa, and immediately flip the paratha so the egg side is now on the base. Let the egg set and cook for 60–90 seconds until golden. Flip once more so the plain side finishes. The finished roti should have a golden egg layer on one side.

Place the cooked paratha on a flat surface, egg side facing up. Spread a drizzle of green chilli sauce across the centre. Spoon a quarter of the paneer filling in a line down the middle. Top with raw sliced onion, a few slivers of green chilli, and fresh coriander leaves if using. Sprinkle with the remaining chaat masala and a squeeze of lemon.

Fold one side of the paratha over the filling, then roll tightly from the bottom up to form a compact cylinder. Wrap the bottom half in foil or parchment paper to hold the roll together and make it easier to eat on the go. Repeat with the remaining parathas. Serve immediately while the roti is still warm and the egg layer is pliable.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: paneer → firm tofu pressed dry and seasoned the same way works well for a dairy-free filling; wheat-flour → store-bought parathas or chapatis save time; cook the egg on top of the ready bread; green-chilli-sauce → sriracha or any chilli hot sauce blended with a teaspoon of vinegar gives a similar kick
